qids.subject: Operating System
qids.prose: Using a larger block size in a fixed block size file system leads to A. better disk throughput but poorer disk space utilization B. better disk throughput and better disk space utilization C. poorer disk throughput but better disk space utilization D. poorer disk throughput and poorer disk space uti

qids.subject: Operating System
qids.prose: A unix-style I-nodes has direct pointers and one single, one double and one triple indirect pointers. Disk block size is Kbyte, disk block address is bits, and -bit integers are used. What is the maximum possible file size? A. bytes B. bytes C. bytes D. bytes

qids.subject: Operating System
qids.prose: What is the swap space in the disk used for? A. Saving temporary html pages B. Saving process data C. Storing the super-block D. Storing device drivers

qids.subject: Operating System
qids.prose: Consider a disk pack with surfaces, tracks per surface and sectors per track. bytes of data are stored in a bit serial manner in a sector. The capacity of the disk pack and the number of bits required to specify a particular sector in the disk are respectively: A. Mbyte, bits B. Mbyte, bits C. Mbyte

qids.subject: Operating System
qids.prose: For a magnetic disk with concentric circular tracks, the seek latency is not linearly proportional to the seek distance due to A. non-uniform distribution of requests
